description.value:
In the court tongue of Svellheim, a promise is a document. A document is a promise. Somewhere in that loop of formalism lies the Binding-Codex — a book purpose-built to hold oaths that cannot be quietly forgotten. The paper remembers. When two parties sign together in the codex, the ink dries red, and both signatories feel a faint awareness thereafter if the other begins to act against the agreement. Not control. Awareness. The rest is politics.

This project produces a leather-bound volume of 40 vellum pages treated with an enchanted iron-gall compound, fitted with iron clasps. When two or more parties sign an oath within the codex — in blood or witnessed ink — the oath becomes supernaturally binding in the following way: neither party can violate the oath without becoming immediately aware that they are doing so, and the other signatory receives a faint but clear impression that something is wrong with the agreement. This does not prevent betrayal, but it removes the possibility of unconscious drift from the terms.

Rules Note: Oaths recorded and signed within the Binding-Codex cannot be broken unknowingly. Both parties feel a distinct mental impression when a violation begins. Deliberate betrayal is still possible, but both parties are fully aware of it in the moment. The codex holds up to 20 separate oaths. Oaths cannot be erased — only formally released by all signatories.

system.prerequisites: 40 sheets of bleached vellum, enchanted iron-gall ink (requires alchemy preparation), iron clasps and binding tools, a Svellspraak court-clerk willing to supply the forming formula.
system.projectSource: Svellspraak Court-Scribe's Compendium — a classified text circulated only among senior court scribes and oath-notaries. Obtaining a copy requires significant favor or considerable forgery skill.

yield.display: Svellspraak binding-codex: 40 vellum pages; oaths signed within cannot be broken unknowingly; all signatories feel violations the moment they begin.
